AR layer for guided tours
Relic Quest

We turned the dead time between tour checkpoints into a treasure hunt — scan hidden artifacts, crack open crystals, and assemble a relic you keep forever.

Dex is Headout’s AI audio-guide app — cinematic, location-aware tours live in New York, London, Paris, Rome, Florence and more. Relic Quest is an AR layer on top of it.

Built on the Dex tour · The Relics
The problem

Tours are one-dimensional.

The status quo is a plain audio guide: you listen at a stop, then disengage on the walk to the next one. We watched it happen — a real Dex guest listened to three tracks, played with the bot and camera, then left. The richest moments, the walk between the highlights, are dead air.

1D

The old tour

Passive. Go through the motions, snap a photo, leave. Nothing earned, nothing kept.

2D

Dex changed it

Our AI-powered immersive audio guide made tours responsive — ask anything, go deeper.

3D

Relic Quest

Now it's interactive. A hunt that leaves you with a souvenir and memories that stick.

The idea
One sentence

A collectible AR hunt layered on top of the tour you already take.

As you move between checkpoints on a Dex audio tour, hidden artifacts wait along the way. Find them, and the tour becomes a game with stakes, surprise, and a reward you assemble piece by piece.

  • Rides a shipping product — Dex is already live and reviewed daily across landmark tours; no new behavior to teach.
  • Dead time becomes the game — the walk between stops is where the magic hides.
How it works · 01 · Find

“There’s something hidden nearby.”

When you pass a hidden artifact, the app nudges you. Tap Look around, raise your phone, and scan the room to find what’s been disguised in plain sight.

  • Curiosity-driven — a prompt, not a chore.
  • It rewards looking closely at a place you’d normally rush past.
How it works · 02 · Come alive

The artifact comes to life.

Scan the right target and it animates in AR — a still wall piece springs into motion and colour, like the object itself woke up. Hold it a moment and a relic drops: Relic unlocked.

  • Video-on-target AR — the artwork moves, plays, transforms.
  • The payoff is immediate — a crystal appears, ready to open.
How it works · 03 · Crack it open

Tap to crack the crystal.

Each relic arrives sealed in a crystal. Tap and it charges with light, fractures, breaks through, and the shards burst away — revealing the 3D relic piece inside. A five-beat ritual that makes every find feel earned.

The hook

One piece of seven.

Every relic is a fragment of a larger badge. Six are hidden across the tour; the seventh — the central, completing piece — only appears when you’ve found all six. Miss one and the badge stays open.

  • Built-in completion drive — the gap pulls you forward.
  • Each piece carries meaning — here, a company principle.
The payoff

The badge completes.

Finish the tour with all seven and the central piece flies in to seal the badge. Tour complete. You didn’t just visit — you finished something, and you have proof.

  • A real souvenir, tied to a specific place and day.
  • Every piece is interactive 3D — rotate it, look closely, and find hidden easter eggs.
  • The emotional close a normal tour never gives you.
Why it travels

Stats worth sharing.

Tour duration, artifacts scanned, questions asked, longest conversation — and surprising moments like “you asked Anne Boleyn about her final words; only 12% did.” The relic and the stats share as one card.

  • Every completed tour becomes a social post.
  • Visitors become the channel — free, organic reach.
It compounds

A shelf of every journey.

Relics live on your profile — Headout, the Louvre, the Colosseum — each a complete badge from a real tour. Tap any one to revisit its stats and share again. A growing trophy case that pulls people back for the next tour.

  • Cross-tour collection — retention you can see.
  • Identity — from explorer to Master Relic Hunter.
The demand is already here

Dex usage is growing ~20% a month — and we layer onto it.

+20%
App opens, month-over-month
514 → 615 / week (last 4 wks)
46%
Of app opens start a tour
1,471 of 3,178 (last 30 days)
↑
Built to lift
dwell time
↑
Built to lift
tours shared to social

Nearly half of everyone who opens Dex commits to a tour, and app opens and tour starts are climbing together (~+20% over four weeks). The behavior we’re enhancing is real and accelerating — Relic Quest deepens it, it doesn’t have to create it.

The opportunity: AR in travel & tourism was ~$20B in 2023 and growing fast; there are ~1.46B active iPhones, and every Dex-capable device is already AR-capable — zero added hardware barrier. Dex’s own roadmap already names games.

What we built · end to end

A complete, working loop — in 42 hours.

Find

AR scan

Hidden artifacts detected and triggered between checkpoints.

Reveal

Video-on-target

The artifact animates in place, then drops a crystal.

Open

Crack-to-3D

Tap-to-shatter reveal of the 3D relic piece inside.

Keep

Badge + profile

Seven-piece assembly, stats, share, cross-tour shelf.

The hunt rides on the existing Dex tour rather than replacing it — which is exactly why it ships.

What’s next

The hunt is just the first map.

Now
Working demo

Full loop running on a real tour, end to end.

Next
Venue pilot

Measure dwell-time and completion lift at one live site.

Then
Authoring tools

Let any venue place its own artifacts and design a badge.

Later
Live artifacts

AR-powered selfies & photo album; conversational AR characters; seasonal & limited relics.

The mechanic is venue-agnostic. Any guided tour with a phone and a wall becomes a hunt — the office demo is just the first map.

Relic Quest
Don’t just visit.
Walk away with it.

We gamified the guided tour — turning a passive walkthrough into an interactive hunt that ends in a souvenir, a story, and a reason to come back.
